harmonized_variables.question: How would you rate the overall economic condition of our country today?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Very bad, 2=Bad, 3=So so (not good nor bad), 4=Good, 5=Very good

harmonized_variables.question: How would you describe the change in the economic condition of our country over the last few years?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Much worse, 2=A little worse, 3=Same, 4=A little better, 5=Much better

harmonized_variables.question: What do you think will be the state of our country's economic condition a few years from now?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Much worse, 2=A little worse, 3=Same, 4=A little better, 5=Much better

harmonized_variables.question: As for your own family, how do you rate the economic situation of your family today?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Very bad, 2=Bad, 3=So so (not good nor bad), 4=Good, 5=Very good

harmonized_variables.question: How would you compare the current economic condition of your family with what it was a few years ago?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Much worse, 2=A little worse, 3=Same, 4=A little better, 5=Much better

harmonized_variables.question: What do you think the economic situation of your family will be a few years from now?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Much worse, 2=A little worse, 3=Same, 4=A little better, 5=Much better

harmonized_variables.question: How fair do you think income distribution is in (country)?
How worried are you that your family might lose its major source of income within the next 12 months?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not worried at all, 2=Not too worried, 3=Somewhat worried, 4=Very worried

harmonized_variables.question: The following is a hypothetical question: If your family were unfortunate enough to lose its main source of income, how serious would it be for your family?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=We would manage just fine, 2=It would be serious but we could deal with it, 3=It would be serious and we would have difficulty coping

harmonized_variables.question: Considering all the effort that you and your family members have made in the past, do you think the income that your family currently receives is fair or not fair?
As compared to my parents' generation, my generation has more or fewer opportuniies to improve one's standard of living or social status?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Substantially fewer opportunities, 2=Fewer opportunities, 3=About the same, 4=More opportunities, 5=Substantially more opportunities

harmonized_variables.question: How much trust do you have in the executive office (president or prime minister)?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=None at all, 2=Not very much trust, 3=Quite a lot of trust, 4=A great deal of trust
harmonized_variables.notes: W5 uses a 6-point scale rather than a 4-point scale; remap accordingly: 1=Trust fully, 2=Trust a lot, 3=Trust somewhat, 4=Distrust somewhat, 5=Distrust a lot, 6=Distrust fully

harmonized_variables.question: How much trust do you have in the courts?

harmonized_variables.question: Did you vote in the [most recent national] election?
Did the respondent vote for the winning camp or losing camp?
Did you attend a campaign meeting or rally?
Did you try to persuade others to vote for a certain candidate or party?
How would you rate the freeness and fairness of the last national election?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not free or fair, 2=Free and fair with major problems, 3=Free and fair with minor problems, 4=Completely free and fair

harmonized_variables.question: How easy or difficult is it to get help from the police when you need it?
Generally speaking, how safe is living in this city/town/village – very safe, safe, unsafe, or very unsafe?
How interested would you say you are in politics?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not at all interested, 2=Not very interested, 3=Somewhat interested, 4=Very interested

harmonized_variables.question: How often do you follow news about politics and government?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Practically never, 2=Not even once a week, 3=Once or twice a week, 4=Several times a week, 5=Everyday

harmonized_variables.question: When you get together with your family members or friends, how often do you discuss political matters?
How close do you feel to [party named]?
Even if parents' demands are unreasonable, children still should do what they ask
Wealth and poverty, success and failure are all determined by fate
A person should not insist on his own opinion if co-workers disagree
For the sake of the family, the individual should put personal interests second
In a group, we should sacrifice our individual interest for the sake of the group's collective interest.
For the sake of national interest, individual interest could be sacrificed.
When dealing with others, developing a long-term relationship is more important than securing one's immediate interest.
When a mother-in-law and a daughter-in-law come into conflict, even if the mother-in-law is in the wrong, the husband should still persuade his wife to obey his mother.
Being a student, one should not question the authority of their teacher.
In a group, we should avoid open quarrel to preserve the harmony of the group.
Even if there is some disagreement with others, one should avoid the conflict.
If one could have only one child, it is more preferable to have a boy than a girl.
Have you contacted elected legislative representatives at any level?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 0=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once (W3); 3=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once (W2)
harmonized_variables.notes: W5 scale is 5-point rather than 3-point: 1=I have done this more than three times, 2=I have done this two or three times, 3=I have done this once, 4=I have not done this but I might do it if something important happens in the future, 5=I have not done this and I would not do it regardless of the situation. W4 scal is 4-point rather than 3-point: 1=I have not done this and I would not do it regardless of the situation, 2=I have not done this but I might do it if something important happens in the future, 3=I have done this once, 4=I have done this more than once. W1 scale is: 9=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once. Remap to 3-point scale accordingly.

harmonized_variables.question: Have you contacted news media?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 0=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once (W3); 3=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once (W2)
harmonized_variables.notes: W5 scale is 5-point rather than 3-point: 1=I have done this more than three times, 2=I have done this two or three times, 3=I have done this once, 4=I have not done this but I might do it if something important happens in the future, 5=I have not done this and I would not do it regardless of the situation. W4 scal is 4-point rather than 3-point: 1=I have not done this and I would not do it regardless of the situation, 2=I have not done this but I might do it if something important happens in the future, 3=I have done this once, 4=I have done this more than once. W1 scale is: 9=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once. Remap to 3-point scale accordingly.

harmonized_variables.question: Have you demonstrated, gone on strike, or sat in for something?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 0=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once (W3); 3=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once (W2)
harmonized_variables.notes: W5 scale is 5-point rather than 3-point: 1=I have done this more than three times, 2=I have done this two or three times, 3=I have done this once, 4=I have not done this but I might do it if something important happens in the future, 5=I have not done this and I would not do it regardless of the situation. W4 scal is 4-point rather than 3-point: 1=I have not done this and I would not do it regardless of the situation, 2=I have not done this but I might do it if something important happens in the future, 3=I have done this once, 4=I have done this more than once. W1 scale is: 9=Never done, 1=Once, 2=More than once. Remap to 3-point scale accordingly.

harmonized_variables.question: Thinking of whether you voted or not ever since you became eligible for voting, how would you describe yourself—have you voted in every election, voted in most elections, voted in some elections or hardly ever voted?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Hardly ever voted, 2=Voted in some elections, 3=Voted in most elections, 4=Voted in every election

harmonized_variables.question: Over the long run, our system of government is capable of solving the problems our country faces.
Thining in general, I am proud of our system of government.
A system like ours, even if it runs into problems, deserves the people's support.
I would rather live under our system of government than any other that I can think of.
Compared with other systems in the world, would you say our system of government works fine as it is, needs minor change, needs major change, or should be replaced?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=It works fine no need to change, 2=Needs minor change, 3=Needs major change, 4=Should be replaced

harmonized_variables.question: On the whole, how satisfied or dissatisfied are you with the way democracy works in our country?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not at all satisfied, 2=Not very satisfied, 3=Fairly satisfied, 4=Very satisfied

harmonized_variables.question: In your opinion, how much of a democracy is [Country Name]?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not a democracy, 2=A democracy with major problems, 3=A democracy with minor problems, 4=A full democracy

harmonized_variables.question: Here is a similar scale of 1 to 10 measuring the extent to which people think democracy is suitable for our country. If "1" means that democracy is completely unsuitable for [name of country] today and "10" means that it is completely suitable, where would you place our country today?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Democracy is completely unsuitable  → 10=Democracy is perfectly suitable

harmonized_variables.question: How satisfied or dissatisfied are you with the [name of president, etc. current ruling] government?
Which statement comes closest: Democracy preferable / Authoritarian sometimes / Doesn't matter?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Democracy is always preferable to any other kind of government, 2=Under some circumstances an authoritarian government can be preferable to a democratic one, 3=For people like me it does not matter
harmonized_variables.notes: For W2, the code is as follows: 1=For people like me it does not matter, 2=Under some circumstances an authoritarian government can be preferable to a democratic one, 3=Democracy is always preferable to any other kind of government

harmonized_variables.question: We should get rid of parliament and elections and have a strong leader decide things
The army should come in to govern the country
We should let experts/technocrats make decisions for the country
Only one political party should be allowed to stand for election and hold office
Between elections, the people have no way of holding the government responsible for its actions.
When government leaders break the laws, there is nothing the court can do.
All citizens from different ethnic communities in [Country X] are treated equally by the government.
Rich and poor people are treated equally by the government.
People have basic necessities like food, clothes, and shelter.
People are free to speak what they think without fear.
People can join any organization they like without fear.
How often do government officials withhold important information from the public?
How often do you think government leaders break the law or abuse their power?
How often do you think our elections offer the voters a real choice between different parties/candidates?
To what extent is the legislature capable of keeping government leaders in check?
How well do you think the government responds to what people want?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not responsive at all, 2=Not very responsive, 3=Largely responsive, 4=Very responsive

harmonized_variables.question: How much do you feel that having elections make the government more responsibe to people's needs?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not at all, 2=Not much, 3=Quite a lot, 4=A good deal

harmonized_variables.question: How widespread do you think corruption and bribe-taking are in your local/municipal government?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Hardly anyone is involved, 2=Not a lot of officials are corrupt, 3=Most officials are corrupt, 4=Almost everyone is corrupt

harmonized_variables.question: How widespread do you think corruption and bribe-taking are in the national government [in capital city]?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Hardly anyone is involved, 2=Not a lot of officials are corrupt, 3=Most officials are corrupt, 4=Almost everyone is corrupt

harmonized_variables.question: Where would you place our country today on a 1-10 democratic scale?
Where would you place our country ten years ago?
Where would you expect our country to be ten years from now?
Where would you place China today on the democratic scale?
Where would you place the United States today on the democratic scale?
Where would you place Japan today on the democratic scale?
Where would you place India today on the democratic scale?
Which of the following statements comes closer to your own view?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Democracy is capable of solving the problems of our society, 2=Democracy cannot solve our society's problems

harmonized_variables.question: If you had to choose between democracy and economic development, which would you say is more important?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Economic development is definitely more important, 2=Economic development is somewhat more important, 3=Democracy is somewhat more important, 4=Democracy is definitely more important

harmonized_variables.question: If you had to choose between reducing economic inequality and protecting political freedom, which would you say is more important?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Reducing economic inequality is definitely more important, 2=Reducing economic inequality is somewhat more important, 3=Protecting political freedom is somewhat more important, 4=Protecting political freedom is definitely more important

harmonized_variables.question: Do you agree or disagree with the following statement: "Democracy may have its problems but it is still the best form of government."
I think I have the ability to participate in politics.
Sometimes politics and government seems so complicated that a person like me can't really understand what is going on.
People like me don't have any influence over what the government does.
You can generally trust the people who run our government to do what is right.
A citizen should always remain loyal only to his country, no matter how imperfect it is or what wrong it has done.
Women should not be involved in politics as much as men
The government should consult religious authorities when interpreting the laws.
People with little or no education should have as much say in politics as highly-educated people.
Government leaders are like the head of a family; we should all follow their decisions.
The government should decide whether certain ideas should be allowed to be discussed in society.
Harmony of the community will be disrupted if people organize lots of groups
When judges decide important cases, they should accept the view of the executive branch.
If the government is constantly checked [i.e. monitored and supervised] by the legislature, it cannot possibly accomplish great things.
If we have political leaders who are morally upright, we can let them decide everything.
If people have too many different ways of thinking, society will be chaotic.
Which country has the most influence in Asia?
In ten years, which country will have the most influence in Asia?
Which country should be a model for own country's future development?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=United States, 2=China, 3=India, 4=Japan, 5=Singapore, 6=Other, 7=We should follow our country's own model

harmonized_variables.question: How much influence does China have on our country?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=No influence at all, 2=Not much influence, 3=Some influence, 4=A great deal of influence

harmonized_variables.question: Generally speaking, the influence China has on our country is?
How much influence does the United States have on our country?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=No influence at all, 2=Not much influence, 3=Some influence, 4=A great deal of influence

harmonized_variables.question: Generally speaking, the influence the United States has on our country is?
How proud are you to be a citizen of this country?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not proud at all, 2=Not very proud, 3=Somewhat proud, 4=Very proud

harmonized_variables.question: Given the chance, how willing would you be to go and live in another country?
Gender of respondent
Year of birth
Age of respondent
Marital status
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Single/Never married, 2=Married, 3=Living-in as married, 4=Widowed, 5=Separated/Married but separated/not living with legal spouse, 6=Divorced

harmonized_variables.question: Highest level of education completed
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=No formal education, 2=Incomplete primary/elementary, 3=Complete primary/elementary, 4=Incomplete secondary/high school (technical/vocational type), 5=Complete secondary/high school (technical/vocational type), 6=Incomplete secondary/high school, 7=Complete secondary/high school, 8=Some university education, 9=University education completed

harmonized_variables.question: How many years of formal education have you received?
Religion of respondent
About how often do you practice religious services or rituals these days?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Practically never, 2=Less often, 3=Only during festivals (or several times a year), 4=Once a month, 5=Once a week, 6=Several times a week, 7=Once a day, 8=Several times a day

harmonized_variables.question: Would you describe yourself as very religious, moderately religious, lightly religious, or not religious at all?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Not religious at all, 2=Lightly religious, 3=Moderately religious, 4=Very religious

harmonized_variables.question: How many people live in this household?
How many generations of family members live in this household?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=One generation, 2=Two generations, 3=Three generations, 4=More than three generations

harmonized_variables.question: Are you currently employed?
What language do you speak the most in the home?
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=Only local language, 2=Mostly local lanugage, 3=A mixture of local and official language, 4=Mostly official language, 5=Only official language

harmonized_variables.question: People sometimes think of the social status of their families in terms of being high or low. Imagine a ladder with 10 steps. At step one stand the lowest status and step 10 stand the highest. Where would you place your family on the following scale?
Imagine a staircase with 10 steps where the poorest people are on the first step and the richest on the tenth step. Where would you put yourself on this staircase?
Imagine a staircase with 10 steps where the poorest people are on the first step and the richest on the tenth step. Where would you put your parents on this staircase?
Imagine a staircase with 10 steps where the poorest people are on the first step and the richest on the tenth step. Where do you think your children will be on this staircase?
Household income level / quintile
harmonized_variables.harmonized_scale: 1=The Fifth Quintile, 2=The Fourth Quintile, 3=The Third Quintile, 4=The Second Quintile, 5=The First Quintile
